classes.reasoning: This document is a peer-reviewed journal article published in the Journal of Neurochemistry (2025; 169:e70176). It is explicitly labeled as a 'REVIEW' article for a special conference issue, with standard academic publishing metadata (DOI, received/revised/accepted dates, peer review history), and includes a full reference list of prior medical literature.

intervention: Anti-amyloid antibodies (lecanemab, donanemab, trontinemab, gantenerumab, MAPTRx)
summary: This is a review article published in the Journal of Neurochemistry discussing the future challenges of Alzheimer's disease treatment, focusing on the recently approved anti-amyloid antibodies (lecanemab and donanemab). The article outlines four major challenges: treatments slow but do not stop disease progression, ARIA (amyloid-related imaging abnormality) side effects, diagnostic accuracy limitations especially in non-white populations, and burdensome administration and safety protocols. It is not a clinical trial document but rather an academic medical publication.
references_to: FDA approval documents for lecanemab and donanemab
